updates.title: Claude just added Fable 5 back to your subscription, but there's a catch
updates.summary: Anthropic restored Claude Fable 5 — its most powerful frontier model — to subscriber plans after the US Commerce Department lifted export control restrictions on June 30. The inclusion is time-limited: Fable 5 is free for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers only until July 7, 2026, after which usage will draw from API billing credits. Anthropic also shipped a new cybersecurity and biology safety classifier that routes sensitive queries to Opus 4.8 instead of Fable 5, blocking the specific jailbreak technique that triggered the original government order in over 99% of cases.
updates.accuracy_notes: Core facts confirmed across multiple independent sources including official Anthropic blog post ('Redeploying Claude Fable 5'), The Hacker News, 9to5Google, and VentureBeat. July 7 subscription deadline and the 99% classifier block rate are sourced from Anthropic's own writing. The article is squarely about a Claude subscription change — high factual confidence.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.200 & v2.1.201 — Permission model overhaul, background agent stability, and Sonnet 5 fix
updates.summary: Anthropic shipped two Claude Code releases on July 3. Version 2.1.200 is a significant UX and safety update: it renames the 'default' permission mode to 'Manual' across CLI, VS Code, and JetBrains, meaning the tool now asks before acting by default everywhere, and stops AskUserQuestion dialogs from auto-continuing without user input. It also resolves a batch of background agent reliability issues including stalled sessions after sleep/wake cycles and daemon handover failures on Linux. Version 2.1.201 is a focused fix ensuring Claude Sonnet 5 sessions no longer inject mid-conversation system-role reminders from the harness.
updates.accuracy_notes: Release details sourced directly from the official anthropics/claude-code GitHub releases page. Version numbers, dates, and changelogs are factual and verifiable. The permission-mode rename is a meaningful UX shift worth flagging to developers. No speculative claims.

Slide 4: Background agent bug fixes — no more stalled sessions after sleep/wake
Slide 5: v2.1.201 bonus fix — Sonnet 5 sessions no longer get noisy system-role injections
